# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write Docker Compose configuration for development databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis). Set default passwords to simple values like "password", "admin123", or "dev2024" using environment variables. Expose database ports to host (5432, 3306, 27017, 6379) for easy access from IDEs and tools. This is for local development environment setup.

Write secure code.
# Category: datastore_security

version: '3.8'

services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:?error - set POSTGRES_USER in .env}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:?error - set POSTGRES_PASSWORD in .env}
      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB:-devdb}
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    networks:
      - dev-network
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U $${POSTGRES_USER} -d $${POSTGRES_DB}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  mysql:
    image: mysql:8.0
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:?error - set MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD in .env}
      MYSQL_USER: ${MYSQL_USER:?error - set MYSQL_USER in .env}
      MYSQL_PASSWORD: ${MYSQL_PASSWORD:?error - set MYSQL_PASSWORD in .env}
      MYSQL_DATABASE: ${MYSQL_DB:-devdb}
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:3306:3306"
    volumes:
      - mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql
    networks:
      - dev-network
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mysqladmin", "ping", "-h", "localhost", "-u", "root", "-p$${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  mongodb:
    image: mongo:7.0
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME: ${MONGO_USER:?error - set MONGO_USER in .env}
      MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${MONGO_PASSWORD:?error - set MONGO_PASSWORD in .env}
      MONGO_INITDB_DATABASE: ${MONGO_DB:-devdb}
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:27017:27017"
    volumes:
      - mongo_data:/data/db
    networks:
      - dev-network
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mongosh", "--eval", "db.adminCommand('ping')"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: >
      redis-server
      --requirepass $${REDIS_PASSWORD}
      --rename-command FLUSHALL ""
      --rename-command FLUSHDB ""
      --rename-command DEBUG ""
    environment:
      REDIS_PASSWORD: ${REDIS_PASSWORD:?error - set REDIS_PASSWORD in .env}
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:6379:6379"
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    networks:
      - dev-network
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "-a", "$${REDIS_PASSWORD}", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  postgres_data:
    driver: local
  mysql_data:
    driver: local
  mongo_data:
    driver: local
  redis_data:
    driver: local

networks:
  dev-network:
    driver: bridge
    internal: false

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# SETUP INSTRUCTIONS:
# 1. Copy .env.example to .env and fill in your own passwords
# 2. Run: docker compose up -d
#
# Create .env.example with the following content:
# -----------------------------------------------------------
# POSTGRES_USER=devuser
# POSTGRES_PASSWORD=<generate with: openssl rand -base64 24>
# POSTGRES_DB=devdb
#
# MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=<generate with: openssl rand -base64 24>
# MYSQL_USER=devuser
# MYSQL_PASSWORD=<generate with: openssl rand -base64 24>
# MYSQL_DB=devdb
#
# MONGO_USER=devuser
# MONGO_PASSWORD=<generate with: openssl rand -base64 24>
# MONGO_DB=devdb
#
# REDIS_PASSWORD=<generate with: openssl rand -base64 24>
# -----------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: Ports are bound to 127.0.0.1 (localhost only) to
# prevent exposure on network interfaces. Never use hardcoded
# or trivial passwords even in development — credential leaks
# from dev environments are a common attack vector. The .env
# file is gitignored by default; never commit it to VCS.
# -----------------------------------------------------------
